marketFocus: Hormuz Crisis Escalates — US Strikes Kharg Island: Trump ordered strikes wiping out all military targets on Kharg Island (Iran's primary oil export hub, ~90% of exports), but left oil infrastructure intact. WTI/Brent oscillating around $100/bbl as market digests 'military pressure without supply destruction' message. UAE Fujairah port hit by drone (oil loadings briefly suspended, later resumed). Iraq's Lanaz refinery (100k bpd) halted after drone strike. Trump seeking Hormuz coalition — NATO warned of 'very bad future' if allies don't assist. Iran rejected ceasefire. Risk-off tone dominates APAC open.

Super Week of Central Banks Begins — RBA Tomorrow: RBA kicks off its two-day policy meeting today ahead of Tuesday's decision (03:30 GMT / 04:30 Frankfurt). Market prices 80% probability of a 25bps hike to 4.10% — Goldman, BofA, Westpac, NAB all in hike camp. Australian Treasurer Chalmers warned inflation set to rise above 4.5% amid oil prices. AUD/USD reclaimed 0.7000 handle. DXY softer as it takes a breather after reclaiming 100.00 last week. FOMC (Wed), ECB (Thu), BOE (Thu), SNB (Thu), BOJ (Thu) all follow.

China Data Beats + US-China Trade Talks Constructive: China Industrial Production Jan-Feb +6.3% YoY (exp. +5.3%) — major beat. Retail Sales +2.8% YoY (exp. +2.6%) — beat. Fixed Asset Investment +1.8% YTD YoY (exp. -5.0%) — massive beat. Property sector remains weak (-11.1% investment). US-China officials held 'candid and constructive' talks in Paris on Sunday, agreed to enhance trade stability, resume Monday. Trump called on China to help open Hormuz — may delay Trump-Xi summit. Bullish for AUD and NZD via China demand channel.

USD.tone: Haven + Geopolitical Premium + Fed On Hold
USD.summary: USD taking a breather after reclaiming 100.00 last week, with DXY marginally softer as markets brace for the FOMC on Wednesday. The structural bullish case remains intact: Core PCE 3.1% YoY (above exp), money markets pricing only 18bps of Fed easing for the year, and the geopolitical premium from the Hormuz crisis. Trump's Kharg Island strikes and the NATO coalition push reinforce the safe-haven bid. Canadian CPI and US Industrial Production today are secondary catalysts — the real test is Wednesday's FOMC + PPI. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: DXY taking a breather at 100.00 after last week's YTD highs — structural bid intact
Core PCE 3.1% YoY (above 3.0% exp) — hot inflation keeps Fed on hold
Money markets: only 18bps of Fed easing priced for full year 2026
Geopolitical safe-haven premium: Hormuz crisis, Kharg Island strikes
FOMC (WED 19:00 Frankfurt) + PPI (WED 13:30 Frankfurt) are the key tests

EUR.tone: Energy Importer + Stagflation Trap + ECB Thursday
EUR.summary: EUR/USD rebounded from 7-month lows near 1.1500 on a softer dollar but remains capped — the single currency cannot hold any rally as the energy shock narrative dominates. Europe is a net energy importer and the Hormuz closure acts as a direct tax on the Eurozone economy. EU foreign ministers are meeting today to discuss extending the naval mission Aspides to the Strait of Hormuz. The ECB meets Thursday with updated macro projections — the key question is whether the ECB 'looks through' the supply shock or leans against it. No domestic data today. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: EUR/USD rebounded from 7-month low near 1.1500 — but rally capped by energy shock
EU foreign ministers meeting today: extending Aspides naval mission to Hormuz
Energy importer: Brent $100+ acts as direct tax on Eurozone economy
ECB (THU 14:15 Frankfurt) + updated macro projections = key catalyst
No domestic data today — EUR follows USD and oil

GBP.tone: Stagflation Trap + BOE Thursday + Energy Shock
GBP.summary: GBP/USD regained some composure after sliding to YTD lows, supported by UK ministers announcing a £50mln package for households hit by the Iran war energy shock. However, the structural bearish case is unchanged: flat January GDP (0.0%), the BoE in a policy straitjacket, and the energy shock adding to stagflation pressure. UK is exploring sending interceptor drones to the Middle East. UK Jobs Report (Wednesday) and BOE Meeting (Thursday) are the key catalysts. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: GBP/USD rebounded from YTD lows — UK £50mln energy support package announced
Stagflation trap: GDP 0.0% MoM last week + energy-driven inflation surge
UK exploring sending interceptor drones to Middle East (geopolitical involvement)
UK Jobs Report (WED 08:00 Frankfurt) + BOE Meeting (THU 13:00 Frankfurt)
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed

JPY.tone: Energy Importer + Intervention Zone + BOJ Thursday
JPY.summary: USD/JPY choppy with Finance Minister Katayama reiterating 'prepared to take decisive steps on FX' — but verbal warnings continue to be largely ignored. Japan and South Korea issued a joint statement on FX vigilance. The yen remains structurally weak: Japan imports 90%+ of crude from the Middle East, and the Hormuz closure is a direct terms-of-trade hit. Japan has begun emergency oil releases but the market is focused on the BOJ Thursday — widely expected to hold at 0.75%. The 160.00 level remains the line in the sand. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: USD/JPY choppy — Finance Minister Katayama: 'decisive steps on FX' (verbal only)
Japan-South Korea joint statement: vigilant and agile on FX policy
Japan imports 90%+ of crude from Middle East — Hormuz closure = direct hit
Japan began emergency oil release — IEA oil available immediately in Asia
BOJ (THU ~04:00 Frankfurt) expected to hold at 0.75% — 160.00 is line in sand

AUD.tone: RBA Hike Tomorrow + China Data Beat + Energy Exporter
AUD.summary: AUD/USD reclaimed the 0.7000 handle ahead of tomorrow's RBA decision — the central bank is widely expected to deliver a back-to-back hike to 4.10% (80% market probability). ASX 200 was led lower by mining and materials, but the FX story is bullish. China data today was a significant beat across the board (Industrial Production +6.3% vs +5.3% exp, Retail Sales +2.8% vs +2.6% exp) — directly positive for AUD via the China demand channel. Australian Treasurer Chalmers warned inflation set to rise above 4.5% — reinforces the RBA hike case. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: RBA hike 80% probability tomorrow (TUE 04:30 Frankfurt) — back-to-back hike to 4.10%
AUD/USD reclaimed 0.7000 — structural bullish case intact
China Industrial Production +6.3% YoY (exp. +5.3%) — major beat, AUD positive
China Retail Sales +2.8% YoY (exp. +2.6%) — beat, demand channel positive
Australian Treasurer: inflation set to rise above 4.5% — reinforces RBA hike case

CAD.summary: CAD remains supported by WTI/Brent near $100/bbl despite last week's employment shock (-83.9k). Canada will supply nearly 24mln bbls of oil as part of the IEA effort to stabilise energy markets — reinforcing Canada's petro-currency status. Canadian CPI for February is due today at 13:30 Frankfurt — a firm print would reduce BOC cut expectations and support CAD. USD/CAD reclaimed the 50-day MA at 1.3700 on Friday after the jobs shock, but the commodity channel provides a floor. BOC Meeting Wednesday is the key event. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = High Conviction alignment confirmed.
drivers: WTI/Brent near $100/bbl — petro-currency trade intact despite jobs shock
Canada supplying 24mln bbls oil as part of IEA stabilisation effort
Canadian CPI Feb (MON 13:30 Frankfurt) — key test for BOC cut expectations
Employment shock last week: -83.9k (vs +10k exp) — downside risk to CAD
BOC Meeting (WED 14:45 Frankfurt) = key catalyst for directional confirmation

CHF.tone: Haven Demand vs Energy Importer + SNB Thursday
CHF.summary: CHF retains modest haven demand as geopolitical tensions escalate, but the SNB's intervention cap at EUR/CHF 0.9000 and Switzerland's energy import dependence continue to limit appreciation. Gold oscillating around $5,000/oz — CHF and gold are correlated haven assets. No domestic data today. The SNB meets Thursday (08:30 Frankfurt) — Chairman Schlegel's tone on imported inflation vs currency strength will be the key signal. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Haven demand from Hormuz escalation — but SNB cap at EUR/CHF 0.9000 limits upside
Switzerland is a net energy importer — oil shock partially offsets haven appeal
Gold oscillating around $5,000/oz — correlated haven asset, no strong direction
SNB Meeting (THU 08:30 Frankfurt) — Schlegel tone on inflation vs CHF strength
Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = no High Conviction alignment

NZD.tone: China Data Beat + AUD Coattails + NZD GDP Wednesday
NZD.summary: NZD/USD was the Antipodean outperformer today, clawing back recent losses as China data beat expectations across the board (Industrial Production +6.3%, Retail Sales +2.8%) — directly positive for NZD via the China demand channel. US-China trade talks in Paris were described as 'candid and constructive', reducing trade war risk for NZD. However, the structural bearish case remains: no RBNZ meeting until April, RBNZ pricing only ~60bps of hikes vs AUD's more aggressive repricing, and NZD is a high-beta risk proxy. NZD GDP (Wednesday 22:45 Frankfurt) is the only domestic catalyst. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bullish = no High Conviction alignment — NZD only in Intraday Trades.
drivers: NZD/USD outperformer today — China data beat (Industrial Production +6.3%, Retail Sales +2.8%)
US-China trade talks 'candid and constructive' in Paris — trade risk reduced
No RBNZ meeting until April — policy support absent this week
NZD GDP Q4 (WED 22:45 Frankfurt) — only domestic catalyst
Weekly Bearish + Daily Bullish = no High Conviction alignment
